title: BIS Stats API Vocabulary
description: Key terms and concepts used in the BIS SDMX RESTful API.

terms.definition: Statistical Data and Metadata eXchange. An ISO standard for the exchange of statistical data and metadata between organisations. The BIS API implements SDMX RESTful API v1.4.0.

A statistical domain or dataset identifier in SDMX. It defines the structure and metadata for a collection of related data. Also referred to as 'flow' in path parameters. Examples: WS_EER (effective exchange rates), WS_CBPOL_D (central bank policy rates).

A combination of dimension values that identifies specific series within a dataflow. Dimensions are separated by dots. Multiple values per dimension are joined with '+'. An empty position acts as a wildcard. Example: M.N.B.CH selects monthly nominal broad basket Switzerland.
The identifier of the maintenance agency responsible for an SDMX artefact. For BIS-maintained artefacts, the agencyID is 'BIS'.
The unique identifier of an SDMX artefact (dataflow, codelist, concept scheme, etc.) within a given agency.
The version number of an SDMX artefact. Use 'latest' to retrieve the current production version. Use 'all' to retrieve all versions.
An SDMX artefact that defines the structure of a dataset, including its dimensions, attributes, and measures.

An SDMX artefact that defines a list of valid codes (enumerated values) for a dimension or attribute. Example codes: frequency values (A=Annual, Q=Quarterly, M=Monthly), country codes (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2).
An SDMX artefact that defines a set of concepts used as dimension or attribute identifiers within data structures.
An SDMX artefact that organises dataflows into a hierarchical classification of statistical subject matter areas.
An SDMX artefact that defines code hierarchies — e.g. geographic regions containing countries.
An SDMX artefact that defines the valid combinations of dimension values for a specific dataflow or data provider.
A derived constraint that represents data availability — which dimension values currently have data — for a specific query selection.
The identifier of a dimension, attribute, or measure within a data structure definition. Used in availability queries to scope availability checks to a single dimension.
The inclusive start of the time range for which data is requested. Accepts ISO 8601 dates (2020-01-01, 2020-01, 2020) or SDMX reporting periods (2020-Q1, 2020-W01, 2020-S1).
The inclusive end of the time range for which data is requested. Same format as startPeriod.
Limits the response to the N oldest observations per series matching the query.
Limits the response to the N most recent observations per series matching the query.

Controls how much information is included in a data response. Values: full (all data and documentation), dataonly (excludes attributes), serieskeysonly (only series keys, no observations), nodata (series with attributes but no observations).
Controls how much information is included in a structure response. Values: allstubs, referencestubs, referencepartial, allcompletestubs, referencecompletestubs, full.
Controls whether referenced SDMX artefacts are included in a structure response. Values: none, parents, parentsandsiblings, children, descendants, all, or a specific artefact type.
Controls the type of availability constraint returned. 'exact' returns values that match existing data; 'available' returns values that remain valid given the current query selection.
HTTP header controlling response compression. Supported values: br, compress, deflate, exi, gzip, identity (default, no compression), pack200-gzip, zstd.

JSON serialisation of SDMX data and structure messages. The BIS API supports SDMX-JSON v1.0.0 for both data (application/vnd.sdmx.data+json;version=1.0.0) and structure (application/vnd.sdmx.structure+json;version=1.0.0) responses.
XML serialisation of SDMX messages. The BIS API supports SDMX-ML 2.1 for both generic data (application/vnd.sdmx.genericdata+xml;version=2.1) and structure-specific data.
Electronic Data Interchange format supported by the BIS API for data (application/vnd.sdmx.data+edi) and structure (application/vnd.sdmx.structure+edi;version=1.0) responses.
